applicant.applicant_address.answer: 412 South Grove Street, Blue Earth, MN 56013

applicant.nature_of_work.answer: Requesting permit for replacement of the existing aging sanitary sewer service line from the single-family residence at 412 South Grove Street to the City main in the street, including installation of a new clean-out, correction of a low spot/sag identified during camera inspection, and disconnection of an old, improperly tied-in footing drain from the sanitary line so that it can be re-routed to the sump system and discharged to the storm sewer as required by current City code. Work will also include temporary excavation in the front yard and boulevard area, traffic control for partial lane closure during the connection, and restoration of disturbed lawn and sidewalk panels as needed.

project.other.answer: Installation of an exterior sanitary clean-out near the front foundation wall for future maintenance access, and abandonment of an old, unused 4" clay sanitary stub discovered near the property line. Contractor will also perform post-construction televised inspection of the new PVC service line and provide a copy of the video and report to the homeowner and the City for their records.

project.project_summary_line_1.answer: The project involves open-cut excavation from the front of the house to the sanitary sewer main in the street to remove approximately 48 feet of original 4" vitrified clay sanitary service pipe that has multiple offset joints, root intrusion, and a low spot causing frequent backups. The line will be replaced with 4" Schedule 40 PVC, properly bedded in sand, with new fittings and a sanitary tee for an exterior clean-out located just outside the foundation wall. The contractor will coordinate with the City to verify location and elevation of the existing main, obtain required inspections prior to backfilling, and ensure the new connection meets current City standards.

project.project_summary_line_2.answer: As part of the work, the existing footing drain that currently ties into the sanitary service inside the basement will be disconnected and routed to the existing sump basket, which will be upgraded with a new 1/2 HP sump pump and discharge line to daylight toward the rear yard, away from the sanitary system in compliance with inflow and infiltration reduction requirements. Any disturbed sidewalk or boulevard area will be restored to City specifications, including replacement of one cracked concrete sidewalk panel if removal is necessary for access. The homeowner has experienced three sanitary backups in the last five years, most recently in April 2025, and a camera inspection on 2025-05-10 documented the sag and root intrusion that this project is intended to permanently correct.

property_owner.property_owner_address.answer: 412 South Grove Street, Blue Earth, MN 56013
